curriculum: Singapore MOE — English, Chinese, Higher Chinese, Literature, PE
notes: Language and humanities syllabus units for all primary/secondary grades

entries.subject: English
entries.syllabusRef: MOE Primary English — Listening and Viewing
keyConcepts: Identify main ideas and supporting details in spoken texts
Infer speakers' purpose, attitude and context from tone and visuals
Take notes from announcements, instructions and short talks
Respond appropriately to questions about viewed media clips
diagramCaptions: Note-taking grid for a school assembly talk
Visual clues in a short video clip

entries.subject: English
entries.syllabusRef: MOE Primary English — Reading and Viewing
keyConcepts: Skim and scan for information in narrative and informational texts
Answer literal and inferential comprehension questions
Interpret visual texts: posters, charts, diagrams with captions
Identify text organisation: headings, bullet points, sequencing
diagramCaptions: Comprehension question types: literal vs inferential
Visual text features on a poster

entries.subject: English
entries.syllabusRef: MOE Primary English — Speaking and Representing
keyConcepts: Plan and deliver a short presentation with clear structure
Use appropriate volume, pace and eye contact
Participate in group discussions: agree, disagree politely, build on ideas
Use visual aids or props to support representing

entries.subject: English
entries.syllabusRef: MOE Primary English — Writing and Representing
keyConcepts: Plan using mind map or paragraph outline before drafting
Write narratives with orientation, complication and resolution
Write emails and formal letters with correct format
Edit for grammar, spelling, punctuation and paragraphing
diagramCaptions: Email format: subject, salutation, body, closing

entries.subject: English
entries.syllabusRef: MOE Primary English — Language Use (Grammar and Vocabulary)
keyConcepts: Use simple, compound and complex sentences correctly
Apply subject-verb agreement and correct tense consistency
Expand vocabulary with synonyms, antonyms and word families
Use connectors: because, although, however, therefore
diagramCaptions: Tense timeline for past, present, future

entries.subject: English
entries.syllabusRef: MOE Lower/Upper Sec English — Visual Text Analysis
keyConcepts: Analyse purpose, audience and context of visual texts
Explain how images, colour, layout and captions create meaning
Compare two visual texts on the same issue
Evaluate bias, stereotypes and persuasive techniques
diagramCaptions: Visual text analysis framework: purpose, audience, techniques

entries.subject: English
entries.syllabusRef: MOE Secondary English — Comprehension and Summary
keyConcepts: Answer literal, inferential and evaluative questions
Write a summary within word limit using own words
Identify tone, irony and writer's attitude
Link ideas across paragraphs using cohesive devices
diagramCaptions: Summary skills: paraphrase without copying phrases
Question command words: explain, suggest, evaluate

entries.subject: English
entries.syllabusRef: MOE Secondary English — Writing
keyConcepts: Plan discursive essays with balanced arguments and rebuttal
Write personal recounts and reflective narratives with voice
Use PEEL / PEELR paragraph structure for expository writing
Edit for register, cohesion and accurate grammar
diagramCaptions: Discursive essay outline: intro, for, against, conclusion

entries.subject: English
entries.syllabusRef: MOE Secondary English — Oral Communication
keyConcepts: Read aloud with appropriate expression and pronunciation
Discuss stimulus picture: describe, interpret, personal response
Respond to examiner questions with extended answers
Use discourse markers to organise spoken responses
diagramCaptions: Oral discussion frame: describe, interpret, evaluate

entries.subject: Chinese
entries.syllabusRef: MOE Primary Chinese — Oral and Listening
keyConcepts: 朗读短文：字音准确、停顿恰当、语气自然
看图说话：描述画面、联系生活、表达观点
听力理解：抓关键词、理解对话与短文主旨
口语互动：提问、回应、补充意见
diagramCaptions: 看图说话三步法：描述、解释、感受

entries.subject: Chinese
entries.syllabusRef: MOE Primary Chinese — Reading
keyConcepts: 理解文章主旨、段落大意与细节信息
推断人物情感、写作意图与语境
积累好词好句，理解常见成语与俗语
初步接触文言文常见虚词与句式
diagramCaptions: 阅读理解题型：主旨、细节、推断

entries.subject: Chinese
entries.syllabusRef: MOE Primary Chinese — Writing
keyConcepts: 记叙文：开头、经过、结尾，细节描写
应用文：书信、通知、日记格式规范
运用比喻、拟人等修辞增强表达
审题、列提纲、修改错字与标点

entries.subject: Chinese
entries.syllabusRef: MOE Primary Chinese — Language Knowledge
keyConcepts: 认读与书写规定字词，区分形近字、音近字
理解词语搭配、近义词与反义词
掌握基本句式：陈述、疑问、感叹、祈使
使用标点符号：逗号、句号、问号、感叹号、引号

entries.subject: Higher Chinese
entries.syllabusRef: MOE Primary Higher Chinese — Character List
keyConcepts: 认读与书写高华字词表中的生字新词
理解多义字、同音字在语境中的用法
积累成语、谚语与名句
辨析近义词语的语体色彩
diagramCaptions: 高华字词分类：认读、书写、运用
sources: MOE 2024 Character List Primary Higher Chinese

entries.subject: Higher Chinese
entries.syllabusRef: MOE Primary Higher Chinese — Reading and Analysis
keyConcepts: 分析文章结构、论证方式与写作特色
文言文初步：常见实词、虚词与翻译
评价作者观点，联系社会现实
比较阅读：同一主题不同文本的异同
diagramCaptions: 文言文阅读三步：释词、串句、概括

entries.subject: Higher Chinese
entries.syllabusRef: MOE Primary Higher Chinese — Writing
keyConcepts: 材料作文：提炼中心、联系材料、结构完整
议论文：论点、论据、论证层次清晰
语言文采：引用名句、对仗、排比
修改润色：逻辑、语病、标点与格式
diagramCaptions: 议论文结构：引论、本论、结论
材料作文审题圈关键词

entries.subject: Literature in Chinese
entries.syllabusRef: MOE Literature in Chinese — Narrative Texts
keyConcepts: 分析人物性格、动机与关系变化
识别叙事视角：第一人称、第三人称、全知视角
理解情节结构：开端、发展、高潮、结局
解读环境描写与象征意义

entries.subject: Literature in Chinese
entries.syllabusRef: MOE Literature in Chinese — Poetry
keyConcepts: 分析意象、意境与情感基调
识别修辞：比喻、拟人、对偶、夸张
理解古诗格律与节奏（初步）
比较同一主题不同诗歌的表达方式

entries.subject: Literature in Chinese
entries.syllabusRef: MOE Literature in Chinese — Drama
keyConcepts: 分析戏剧冲突与人物台词背后的潜台词
理解舞台说明、场景转换与戏剧结构
讨论主题思想与社会背景
比较阅读剧本与演出效果的差异
diagramCaptions: 戏剧冲突三角：人物、目标、障碍

entries.subject: Literature in Chinese
entries.syllabusRef: MOE Literature in Chinese — Appreciation and Response
keyConcepts: 写出有观点的文学评论：引证原文、分析、评价
比较不同作品的主题与写作技巧
联系作者生平与时代背景理解文本
运用文学术语准确表达（如讽刺、象征、对比）

entries.subject: Physical Education
entries.syllabusRef: MOE PE Primary — Movement Education
keyConcepts: Locomotor skills: run, jump, hop, skip with control
Non-locomotor: balance, twist, stretch, turn
Manipulative skills: throw, catch, strike, dribble
Apply skills in modified games and cooperative activities

entries.subject: Physical Education
entries.syllabusRef: MOE PE Primary — Health and Fitness Education
keyConcepts: Components of fitness: endurance, strength, flexibility
Warm-up and cool-down routines and why they matter
Healthy habits: hydration, sleep, balanced activity
Measure and track personal fitness goals safely

entries.subject: Physical Education
entries.syllabusRef: MOE PE Secondary — Athletics
keyConcepts: Sprint and middle-distance running technique
Jumping events: take-off, flight, landing phases
Throwing events: grip, rotation, release angle
Training principles: overload, progression, recovery

entries.subject: Physical Education
entries.syllabusRef: MOE PE Secondary — Games and Sports
keyConcepts: Offensive and defensive principles in invasion games
Court/field positioning and communication in team sports
Umpiring, fair play and sportsmanship
Analyse performance using video or checklist feedback
diagramCaptions: Team roles in a basketball half-court
